enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each record where RNEXT==RNAME: the '=' alias and explicit RNAME are semantically equivalent per SAMv1 §1.4.7, so all parsed fields (getMateReferenceName, getReadPairedFlag, etc.) must be identical after round-tripping through the parser

evidence.quote: This field is set as `*' when the information is unavailable, and set as `=' if RNEXT is identical RNAME.

enforced.oracle: Uppercasing SEQ is semantics-preserving per SAMv1 §1.4.10 ('No assumptions can be made on the letter cases'); all parsed fields (getReadString, getReadLength, getBaseQualityString) must be identical between x and T(x) after parsing, modulo case normalization of the read string

evidence.quote: No assumptions can be made on the letter cases.

enforced.mr_name: Flag bit 0x4 exclusivity violation on mapped read
enforced.oracle: A compliant parser MUST reject (raise validation error, crash, or refuse to emit) a record where FLAG bit 0x4 (segment unmapped) is set but RNAME is not '*' and POS > 0. The error-consensus oracle checks that all SUTs vote REJECT or CRASH; any ACCEPT or SILENT_SKIP is a conformance bug.

evidence.quote: Bit 0x4 is the only reliable place to tell whether the read is unmapped. If 0x4 is set, no assumptions can be made about RNAME, POS, CIGAR, MAPQ, and bits 0x2, 0x100, and 0x800.

RNAME: Reference sequence NAME of the alignment. ... An unmapped segment without coordinate has a '*' at this field. ... POS: 1-based leftmost mapping POSition ... POS is set as 0 for an unmapped read without coordinate.

enforced.oracle: A compliant parser MUST reject a record where the sum of query-consuming CIGAR operations (M, I, S, =, X) does not equal len(SEQ) when SEQ != '*'. The error-consensus oracle checks that all SUTs vote REJECT or CRASH; any ACCEPT or SILENT_SKIP is a conformance bug.

evidence.quote: SEQ: segment SEQuence. This field can be a '*' when the sequence is not stored. If not a '*', the length of the sequence must equal the sum of lengths of MIS=X operations in CIGAR.

enforced.mr_name: TLEN sign consistency violation for paired reads
enforced.oracle: A compliant parser MUST reject a paired-end template where both reads carry TLEN values with the same sign (both positive or both negative) when both mates are mapped to the same reference. The error-consensus oracle checks that all SUTs vote REJECT or CRASH; any ACCEPT or SILENT_SKIP is a conformance bug.

evidence.quote: The TLEN field is positive for the leftmost segment of the template, negative for the rightmost, and the sign for any middle segment is undefined. If segments cover the same coordinates then the choice of which is leftmost and rightmost is arbitrary, but the two ends must still have differing signs.

enforced.mr_name: RNEXT equals alias invariance under query methods
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method — RNEXT '=' alias is semantically equivalent to explicit RNAME per SAMv1 §1.4.7, so all query methods must return identical values

evidence.quote: RNEXT: Reference sequence name of the primary alignment of the NEXT read in the template. ... This field is set as `*' when the information is unavailable, and set as `=' if RNEXT is identical RNAME.

enforced.mr_name: SEQ case normalization invariance under query methods
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method — SEQ letter case is not semantically significant per SAMv1 §1.4.10, so query methods returning derived values must be invariant

evidence.quote: SEQ: segment SEQuence. ... No assumptions can be made on the letter cases.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM optional tag field order invariance
quarantine.oracle: After permuting the order of optional TAG:TYPE:VALUE fields on each alignment record, the parsed SAM object must deep-equal the original. The order of optional fields is not significant per SAMv1 §1.5.

evidence.quote: Within each alignment line, no TAG may appear more than once and the order in which the optional fields appear is not significant.
Within each alignment line, no TAG may appear more than once and the order in which the optional fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.oracle: After shuffling the TAG:VALUE pairs inside the @HD header line, the parsed SAM object must deep-equal the original. The order of fields within a header line is not significant per SAMv1 §1.3.

evidence.quote: Within each (non-@CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.
Within each (non-@CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.oracle: After shuffling the TAG:VALUE pairs inside each @SQ header line, the parsed SAM object must deep-equal the original. The order of fields within a header line is not significant per SAMv1 §1.3.

evidence.quote: Within each (non-@CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.
Within each (non-@CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.oracle: After shuffling the TAG:VALUE pairs inside each @RG header line, the parsed SAM object must deep-equal the original. The order of fields within a header line is not significant per SAMv1 §1.3.

evidence.quote: Within each (non-@CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.
Within each (non-@CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.oracle: After shuffling the TAG:VALUE pairs inside each @PG header line, the parsed SAM object must deep-equal the original. The order of fields within a header line is not significant per SAMv1 §1.3.

evidence.quote: Within each (non-@CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.
Within each (non-@CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.oracle: After shuffling the order of @CO comment lines in the header, the parsed SAM object must deep-equal the original. @CO lines are free-text comments with no ordering semantics per SAMv1 §1.3.

evidence.quote: In the header, each line is TAB-delimited and, apart from @CO lines, each data field follows a format `TAG:VALUE'
In the header, each line is TAB-delimited and, apart from @CO lines, each data field follows a format `TAG:VALUE'

quarantine.oracle: After reordering @SQ and @RG header lines among themselves (while keeping @HD first), the parsed SAM object must deep-equal the original. The spec does not mandate a specific order among header record types beyond @HD being first.

evidence.quote: Each header line begins with the character `@' followed by one of the two-letter header record type codes defined in this section.
@HD & File-level metadata. Optional. If present, there must be only one @HD line and it must be the first line of the file.

ambiguity_flags: The spec only mandates @HD as first line; order among @SQ, @RG, @PG is not explicitly constrained, but some tools may assume a particular order.

quarantine.oracle: Splitting or merging adjacent identical CIGAR operations (e.g., 10M -> 4M6M) preserves total query-consumed and reference-consumed lengths; the parsed alignment coordinates (getAlignmentStart, getAlignmentEnd, getLengthOnReference) and read sequence must be identical between x and T(x)

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p134
evidence.quote: Adjacent CIGAR operations should be different.
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

ambiguity_flags: The spec says 'should' (ADVISORY), not 'must', so adjacent identical ops are not strictly forbidden but normalization tools may merge/split them

quarantine.mr_name: CIGAR zero-length op removal normalization invariance
quarantine.oracle: Zero-length CIGAR operations carry no alignment information; removing them and merging adjacent same-type ops preserves the alignment coordinates (getAlignmentStart, getAlignmentEnd, getCigarLength, getLengthOnReference) and read sequence

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p134
evidence.quote: Adjacent CIGAR operations should be different.
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

ambiguity_flags: The spec does not explicitly forbid zero-length CIGAR ops; the recommendation only says adjacent ops 'should' be different (ADVISORY)

quarantine.oracle: When FLAG bit 0x4 (unmapped) is set, MAPQ should be 255 ('not available') per SAMv1 §1.4; normalizing MAPQ to 255 for unmapped reads preserves the semantics of the alignment record (getReadUnmappedFlag, getMappingQuality after normalization to 255)

evidence.quote: Bit 0x4 is the only reliable place to tell whether the read is unmapped. If 0x4 is set, no assumptions can be made about RNAME, POS, CIGAR, MAPQ, and bits 0x2, 0x100, and 0x800.

ambiguity_flags: MAPQ 255 is defined as 'not available' but the spec does not mandate that unmapped reads MUST have MAPQ=255; it says 'no assumptions can be made'

quarantine.oracle: A compliant parser MUST reject a record where an optional tag's TYPE character is not one of the valid types (A, i, f, Z, H, B). The error-consensus oracle checks that all SUTs vote REJECT or CRASH; any ACCEPT or SILENT_SKIP is a conformance bug.

evidence.quote: All optional fields follow the TAG:TYPE:VALUE format where TAG is a two-character string that matches /[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9]/. ... In an optional field, TYPE is a single case-sensitive letter which defines the format of VALUE:
String fields and hex-formatted byte arrays are represented as NUL-terminated text strings:

quarantine.mr_name: POS shift with matching LN extension preserves alignment validity
quarantine.oracle: After shifting all POS values by +shift and increasing corresponding @SQ LN by +shift, the alignment end (POS + CIGAR ref-consumed bases) must still be <= LN for every mapped record. The getAlignmentStart(), getAlignmentEnd(), getReferenceName() query methods must return identical values before and after the transform.

evidence.quote: POS: 1-based leftmost mapping POSition of the first CIGAR operation that ``consumes'' a reference base (see table below). The first base in a reference sequence has coordinate 1. POS is set as 0 for an unmapped read without coordinate.
LN* & Reference sequence length. Range: $[1,\,2^31-1]$

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p138
evidence.quote: If POS plus the sum of lengths of M=XDN operations in CIGAR exceeds the length specified in the LN field of the @SQ header line (if exists) with an SN equal to RNAME, the alignment should be unmapped, unless the reference sequence is circular (see below).
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

ambiguity_flags: The spec says POS+ref_consumed > LN 'should' (not 'must') mark the alignment unmapped — this is advisory, not mandatory. Some parsers may accept such records silently.

quarantine.mr_name: BAM round-trip preserves 1-based POS coordinate semantics
quarantine.oracle: After SAM→BAM→SAM round-trip, every mapped alignment record's POS must remain unchanged (1-based coordinate preserved). The BIN field recomputation must be consistent with the original POS. For unmapped reads with POS=0, the round-trip must preserve POS=0.

evidence.quote: BIN is calculated using the reg2bin() function... For mapped reads this uses -1 (i.e., 0-based left position) and the alignment end point using the alignment length from the CIGAR string.

POS: 1-based leftmost mapping POSition of the first CIGAR operation that ``consumes'' a reference base... The first base in a reference sequence has coordinate 1. POS is set as 0 for an unmapped read without coordinate.

ambiguity_flags: BAM internally stores POS-1 (0-based), so the round-trip must correctly convert back to 1-based. Some implementations may have off-by-one errors in this conversion.

quarantine.mr_name: CRAM round-trip preserves POS coordinate and alignment end
quarantine.oracle: After SAM→CRAM→SAM round-trip, every mapped alignment record's POS must remain unchanged. The alignment end (POS + CIGAR ref-consumed bases - 1) must be preserved. For unmapped reads with POS=0, the round-trip must preserve POS=0.

evidence.quote: POS: 1-based leftmost mapping POSition of the first CIGAR operation that ``consumes'' a reference base... The first base in a reference sequence has coordinate 1. POS is set as 0 for an unmapped read without coordinate.

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p138
evidence.quote: If POS plus the sum of lengths of M=XDN operations in CIGAR exceeds the length specified in the LN field of the @SQ header line (if exists) with an SN equal to RNAME, the alignment should be unmapped, unless the reference sequence is circular (see below).
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

ambiguity_flags: CRAM may collapse =/X CIGAR ops to M, which changes CIGAR but not the reference span. The oracle must account for this via canonical normalization.

quarantine.mr_name: Query method invariance for coordinate getters under header reordering
quarantine.oracle: After reordering @SQ header lines, the coordinate-related query methods (getAlignmentStart, getAlignmentEnd, getStart, getEnd, getReferenceName) on each parsed alignment record must return identical values before and after the transform. The reference index (getReferenceIndex) may change if @SQ order changes, but the reference name and coordinates must be invariant.

evidence.quote: POS: 1-based leftmost mapping POSition of the first CIGAR operation that ``consumes'' a reference base... The first base in a reference sequence has coordinate 1.
SN* & Reference sequence name. The SN tags and all individual AN names in all @SQ lines must be distinct. The value of this field is used in the alignment records in RNAME and RNEXT fields.

ambiguity_flags: getReferenceIndex returns the ordinal position in the @SQ dictionary, which changes when @SQ lines are reordered. The oracle must compare by reference name, not index.

quarantine.mr_name: Header @HD VN version bump preserves parsed content
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) — Toggling @HD VN between 1.5 and 1.6 is backwards-compatible per SAM version history; no 1.6-only features are used in the test corpus

evidence.quote: This specification is for version 1.6 of the SAM and BAM formats. Each SAM and BAM file may optionally specify the version being used via the @HD VN tag.

ambiguity_flags: VN field is optional per spec; some parsers may reject unrecognized version strings

quarantine.mr_name: Optional tag permutation invariance under query methods
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method — optional field order is not significant per SAMv1 §1.4, so all mandatory-field query methods must return identical values

evidence.quote: Within each alignment line, no TAG may appear more than once and the order in which the optional fields appear is not significant.
